Title: Create new loyalty program
Preconditions: Admin user logged in; Loyalty module accessible at #!/workspace/loyalty
Steps:
[NAV] {{BACK_URL}}/#!/workspace/loyalty
[BLADE] loyalty programs list blade is visible
[ACT] click 'Add' button in toolbar
[BLADE] new loyalty program detail blade opens
[ACT] fill 'Name': 'Test Loyalty Program'
[ACT] select 'Store': @td(STORE_PRIMARY.id) from dropdown
[ACT] fill 'Priority': 1
[ACT] toggle 'Active' to ON
[ACT] fill 'Start date' to today's date
[SAVE] click 'Save'
Assertions:
[TOAST] success notification displayed
[FORM] program appears in loyalty programs list
[FORM] 'Name' field shows 'Test Loyalty Program'
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] no JS errors during save
[NETWORK] no 4xx/5xx on save request
Cleanup: Delete the created test loyalty program after test

Title: View loyalty program details
Preconditions: Admin user logged in; at least one loyalty program exists
Steps:
[NAV] {{BACK_URL}}/#!/workspace/loyalty
[GRID] locate any existing program in list
[ACT] click on program row
[BLADE] program detail blade opens
Assertions:
[FORM] fields displayed: Active toggle; Priority; Name; Localized names; Store; Start date; End date
[BLADE] Conditions section visible
[BLADE] Rewards section visible

Title: Edit existing loyalty program
Preconditions: Admin user logged in; at least one loyalty program exists

[ACT] click on existing program
[BLADE] detail blade opens
[ACT] fill 'Name': 'Updated Program Name'
[ACT] fill 'Priority': 10
[ACT] toggle 'Active' to opposite state
[SAVE] click 'Save'
[ACT] close blade
[ACT] click the program again
[BLADE] detail blade reopens
Assertions:

[FORM] 'Priority' shows 10
[FORM] 'Active' reflects toggled state

Title: Delete loyalty program

Title: Add single condition to program
Preconditions: Admin user logged in; a loyalty program is open for editing

[ACT] open existing program or create new one
[BLADE] program detail blade visible
[ACT] click 'Add condition' in Conditions section
[WAIT] 3-group condition menu appears (Shopper profile / Order conditions / Special conditions)
[ACT] click 'Order conditions' group to expand
[ACT] click 'Order status is...' condition row
[WAIT] condition row added with status selector
[ACT] click 'Add' inline link next to the status selector
[ACT] select 'Completed' from the status picker
[SAVE] click 'Save'
[ACT] close and reopen program
Assertions:
[FORM] condition 'Order status' visible in conditions list with value 'Completed'
[FORM] condition persisted after reopen

Title: Add multiple conditions to program

[ACT] open existing program
[ACT] click 'Add condition'; expand 'Order conditions' group; click 'Order status is...'; click 'Add' link; select 'Completed'
[ACT] click 'Add condition' again; expand 'Order conditions' group; click 'Order status is...'; click 'Add' link; select 'Pending'
[SAVE] click 'Save'
[ACT] close and reopen program

[FORM] both conditions visible in conditions section
[FORM] logical operator 'If any of the following criteria' applies to all

Title: Admin SPA — Configure loyalty settings on Store blade (happy path)
Preconditions:
[PRE:CLEAR_SESSION]
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:ADMIN]
Admin user logged in with loyalty:update permission; Loyalty module installed; store @td(STORE_PRIMARY.id) exists in Admin SPA
Steps:
[NAV] {{BACK_URL}}/#!/workspace/stores
[BLADE] Stores list blade visible
[ACT] click on store @td(STORE_PRIMARY.id) in grid
[BLADE] Store detail blade opens
[WAIT] blade fully loaded; widgets visible
[ACT] scroll to and click 'Loyalty settings' widget (lowercase s)
[BLADE] 'loyalty-setting-details' blade opens
[ACT] toggle 'Enable Loyalty' to ON
[ACT] click 'Loyalty Mode' dropdown
[ACT] select 'Mixed Cart'
[ACT] click 'Loyalty Currency' dropdown; select @td(LOYALTY_SETTINGS.currency_code) from the store currencies list
[SAVE] click 'Save'
[BLADE] blade closes or shows success
[ACT] reopen 'Loyalty settings' widget on store blade
Assertions:
[TOAST] success notification after save
[FORM] 'Enable Loyalty' toggle shows ON after reopen
[FORM] 'Loyalty Mode' shows 'Mixed Cart' after reopen
[FORM] 'Loyalty Currency' shows @td(LOYALTY_SETTINGS.currency_code) after reopen
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] GET {{BACK_URL}}/api/loyalty-setting/store/@td(STORE_PRIMARY.id) returns HTTP 200
[API] response body loyaltyEnabled=true; loyaltyMode='Mixed Cart'; loyaltyCurrency=@td(LOYALTY_SETTINGS.currency_code) (extra fields such as loyaltyRoute are tolerated)
[CONSOLE] no JS errors in Admin SPA
Failure_Signals: Spinner visible >5s; blade fails to open; save toast shows error; reopen shows stale values
Cleanup: Restore original loyalty settings via PUT {{BACK_URL}}/api/loyalty-setting

Title: Mode dropdown shows exactly the four allowed values
Preconditions: state from LOY-019 (Admin logged in; Store blade open; Loyalty Settings blade open)
Steps:
[NAV] {{BACK_URL}}/#!/workspace/stores
[ACT] open store @td(STORE_PRIMARY.id)
[ACT] open 'Loyalty Settings' widget
[BLADE] 'loyalty-setting-details' blade opens
[ACT] click 'Loyalty Mode' dropdown
[WAIT] dropdown options rendered
Assertions:
[FORM] dropdown shows exactly 4 options: 'Loyalty Store'; 'Mixed Cart'; 'Coupon Redemption'; 'Payment Method'
[FORM] no extra or missing options beyond the 4 AllowedValues
Failure_Signals: Dropdown fails to open; spinner visible >3s

Title: API GET returns configured loyalty settings for store
Preconditions: Loyalty settings for store @td(STORE_PRIMARY.id) are configured: loyaltyEnabled=true; loyaltyMode='Mixed Cart'; loyaltyCurrency=@td(LOYALTY_SETTINGS.currency_code). If not yet configured; run the PUT from LOY-022 pattern first (FIXTURE-SEEDING).
Steps:
[AUTH] obtain admin Bearer token via POST {{BACK_URL}}/connect/token (grant_type=password; username={{ADMIN_EMAIL}}; password={{ADMIN_PASSWORD}})
[HTTP] GET {{BACK_URL}}/api/loyalty-setting/store/@td(STORE_PRIMARY.id) with Authorization: Bearer <token>
Assertions:
[STATUS] HTTP 200
[BODY] storeId == @td(STORE_PRIMARY.id)
[BODY] loyaltyEnabled == true
[BODY] loyaltyMode == 'Mixed Cart'
[BODY] loyaltyCurrency == @td(LOYALTY_SETTINGS.currency_code)
[BODY] additional fields (e.g. loyaltyRoute) may be present — tolerated; do NOT assert them absent
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[ADMIN] values match what was set in Admin SPA (cross-reference LOY-019)
[CONSOLE] no JS errors
Failure_Signals: HTTP 4xx/5xx; storeId missing from response; loyaltyEnabled not present

Title: API PUT updates loyalty settings — round-trip verification
Preconditions: Admin Bearer token obtainable; store @td(STORE_PRIMARY.id) exists
Assertions:
[STATUS] PUT returns HTTP 204 No Content
[BODY] GET response loyaltyMode == 'Loyalty Store'
[BODY] GET response loyaltyEnabled == true
[BODY] GET response loyaltyCurrency == @td(LOYALTY_SETTINGS.currency_code)
[BODY] additional fields (e.g. loyaltyRoute) may be present — tolerated
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[ADMIN] Admin SPA Loyalty Settings blade reflects 'Loyalty Store' after reload
[NETWORK] no 4xx/5xx on PUT or GET
Failure_Signals: PUT returns non-204; GET after PUT still shows old mode; 500 error
Cleanup: Restore mode to 'Mixed Cart' via PUT after test

Title: API PUT without loyalty:update permission returns 403
Preconditions: A storefront user token exists (user lacks loyalty:update platform permission)
Steps:
[AUTH] obtain token for user WITHOUT loyalty:update permission (e.g. storefront user via {{USER_EMAIL}}/{{USER_PASSWORD}})
[HTTP] PUT {{BACK_URL}}/api/loyalty-setting with body: {storeId: @td(STORE_PRIMARY.id); loyaltyEnabled: false; loyaltyMode: 'Coupon Redemption'; loyaltyCurrency: @td(LOYALTY_SETTINGS.currency_code)}
Assertions:
[STATUS] HTTP 403 Forbidden
[BODY] error or empty body (NOT 204)
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] subsequent GET still returns original values — settings NOT changed
[NETWORK] no 500 error
Failure_Signals: HTTP 200 returned (permission bypass); 500 error instead of 403

Title: API GET with unknown storeId returns null or empty
Preconditions: Admin Bearer token obtainable
Steps:
[AUTH] obtain admin Bearer token
[HTTP] GET {{BACK_URL}}/api/loyalty-setting/store/NON_EXISTENT_STORE_XYZ_99999
Assertions:
[STATUS] HTTP 200
[BODY] response is null or empty object (NOT a 404 or 500)

Title: Settings propagate to storefront xAPI store query
Preconditions: Loyalty settings saved: Enable=true; Mode=Mixed Cart; Currency=@td(LOYALTY_SETTINGS.currency_code) for store @td(STORE_PRIMARY.id) (established by LOY-019)
Steps:
[AUTH] obtain Bearer token (storefront user or anonymous)
[HTTP] POST {{BACK_URL}}/graphql body: {"query": "{ store(storeId: \"@td(STORE_PRIMARY.id)\", cultureName: \"{{CULTURE_NAME}}\") { settings { modules { moduleId settings { name value } } } } }"}
Assertions:
[STATUS] HTTP 200
[BODY] response contains module with moduleId='VirtoCommerce.Loyalty'
[BODY] within that module settings[name='Loyalty.Enable'] value == true
[BODY] within that module settings[name='Loyalty.Mode'] value == 'Mixed Cart'
[BODY] within that module settings[name='Loyalty.Currency'] value == @td(LOYALTY_SETTINGS.currency_code)
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] errors[] is empty (HTTP 200 does NOT guarantee xAPI success)
[NETWORK] no 4xx/5xx on /graphql
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty; module 'VirtoCommerce.Loyalty' absent from modules array; currency value empty

Title: Per-Product Loyalty Factor — Set factor=2 via Product Points Loyalty program and verify persistence
Section: Loyalty > Product Points Loyalty > Product factors
Preconditions:
[PRE:CLEAR_SESSION]
VERIFIED 2026-05-29 (Loyalty 3.1003.0): Product factors live under a Product Points Loyalty program; the program's AUDIENCE is set by a rule-tree condition (Add condition > "Shopper profile" > "User group is ..." or "Any User Group"). Per-customer-group factors are modeled as ONE program per group (each program has its own Product factors); matching = customer user group -> matching program -> that program's product factor; no matching program/group -> default. Path to factors: Loyalty > Add > "Product Points Loyalty" > fill Name/Store (+ add a "User group is..."/"Any User Group" condition for audience) > Save > "Product factors" widget appears at bottom of the program blade > open it > Add > "Select Catalog items..." picker (columns Pic/Name/SKU) > check product > "Add selected" > factor grid columns Product name | SKU | Factor (spinbutton, default 1) > Save. REST: /api/loyalty-program-product-factors (model {loyaltyProgramId, productId, factor}); group lives on the program conditions, not the factor row. Admin logged in; at least one product exists in catalog visible in the product picker.
Steps:
[NAV] {{BACK_URL}}/#!/workspace/loyalty
[BLADE] 'Loyalty programs' list blade is visible
[ACT] click 'Add' button in the toolbar
[BLADE] 'Add loyalty program' type-selector blade opens
[ACT] click the 'Product Points Loyalty' type (subtitle: 'Reward customers with points for purchasing specific products')
[BLADE] 'New loyalty program' detail blade opens — note the banner 'Save the program first to start managing product factors'
[ACT] fill 'Name' with a unique value (prefix AGENT-TEST-)
[ACT] select 'Store': @td(STORE_PRIMARY.id) from the Store dropdown
[SAVE] click 'Save'
[BLADE] after save, the 'Product factors' widget (count 0) appears at the bottom of the program blade
[ACT] click the 'Product factors' widget
[BLADE] 'Product factors' blade opens (grid empty — 'No data')
[ACT] click 'Add' in the Product factors blade toolbar
[BLADE] 'Select Catalog items...' product picker opens
[ACT] navigate to a category and check one product row
[ACT] click 'Add selected'
[FORM] product row appears in the Product factors grid with a 'New' badge and Factor defaulting to 1
[ACT] set the Factor spinbutton to 2
[SAVE] click 'Save' in the Product factors blade
[ACT] click 'Refresh' (or close and reopen the Product factors blade)
Assertions:
[FORM] factor row persists after save — product name + SKU visible with Factor=2
[FORM] 'New' badge clears after successful save
[TOAST] success notification after save
[API] POST /api/loyalty-program-product-factors/search returns the row with factor=2 for the saved program
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] no JS errors during save
[NETWORK] no 4xx/5xx on the factor save request
[API] /api/loyalty-program-product-factors/search returns factor=2
Failure_Signals: Spinner >10s after save; toast error; factor row absent after reopen; factor reverts to 1; 4xx/5xx on save
Cleanup: Delete the Product factors row, then delete the AGENT-TEST- program (DELETE /api/loyalty-programs?ids=… cascades the factor)

Title: Per-Product Factor — No matching program/user-group falls back to default factor
Section: Loyalty > Product Points Loyalty > Product factors
Preconditions:
[PRE:CLEAR_SESSION]
VERIFIED 2026-05-29 (Loyalty 3.1003.0): Product factors live under a Product Points Loyalty program; the program's AUDIENCE is set by a rule-tree condition (Add condition > "Shopper profile" > "User group is ..." or "Any User Group"). Per-customer-group factors are modeled as ONE program per group (each program has its own Product factors); matching = customer user group -> matching program -> that program's product factor; no matching program/group -> default. Path to factors: Loyalty > Add > "Product Points Loyalty" > fill Name/Store (+ add a "User group is..."/"Any User Group" condition for audience) > Save > "Product factors" widget appears at bottom of the program blade > open it > Add > "Select Catalog items..." picker (columns Pic/Name/SKU) > check product > "Add selected" > factor grid columns Product name | SKU | Factor (spinbutton, default 1) > Save. REST: /api/loyalty-program-product-factors (model {loyaltyProgramId, productId, factor}); group lives on the program conditions, not the factor row. Title: Per-Customer-Group Factor — VIP-scoped program applies its factor to a VIP user; non-matching user falls back to default
Section: Loyalty > Product Points Loyalty > User group condition + Product factors
Preconditions:
[PRE:CLEAR_SESSION]
VERIFIED 2026-05-29 (Loyalty 3.1003.0): Product factors live under a Product Points Loyalty program; the program's AUDIENCE is set by a rule-tree condition (Add condition > "Shopper profile" > "User group is ..." or "Any User Group"). Per-customer-group factors are modeled as ONE program per group (each program has its own Product factors); matching = customer user group -> matching program -> that program's product factor; no matching program/group -> default. Path to factors: Loyalty > Add > "Product Points Loyalty" > fill Name/Store (+ add a "User group is..."/"Any User Group" condition for audience) > Save > "Product factors" widget appears at bottom of the program blade > open it > Add > "Select Catalog items..." picker (columns Pic/Name/SKU) > check product > "Add selected" > factor grid columns Product name | SKU | Factor (spinbutton, default 1) > Save. REST: /api/loyalty-program-product-factors (model {loyaltyProgramId, productId, factor}); group lives on the program conditions, not the factor row. VIP user group exists (confirmed selectable: contract-contract2 / Wholesaler / VIP). VIP-group customer user provisioned: @td(LOYALTY_VIP_USER.email) (memberId @td(LOYALTY_VIP_USER.memberId), groups=['VIP'], storefront auth confirmed 2026-05-29). A non-VIP / no-group customer user also exists; a product visible in the picker.
Steps:
[NAV] {{BACK_URL}}/#!/workspace/loyalty
[ACT] click 'Add' > select 'Product Points Loyalty'
[ACT] fill 'Name' (prefix AGENT-TEST-, e.g. AGENT-TEST-VIP-Program) and select 'Store': @td(STORE_PRIMARY.id)
[ACT] in the 'Loyalty program' rule-tree, click 'Add condition' > 'Shopper profile' > 'User group is ...'
[ACT] in the condition row click 'Add' and select the @td(USER_GROUP_VIP.name) group from the dropdown (chip shows: User group is "VIP")
[SAVE] click 'Save'
[ACT] open the 'Product factors' widget > 'Add' > pick the test product > 'Add selected' > set Factor (e.g. 6) > 'Save'
[API] obtain a storefront token for the VIP user: POST {{BACK_URL}}/connect/token grant_type=password&scope=offline_access&username=@td(LOYALTY_VIP_USER.email)&password=@td(LOYALTY_VIP_USER.password)&storeId=@td(STORE_PRIMARY.id)
[API] as the VIP user, POST {{BACK_URL}}/graphql: products(storeId:"@td(STORE_PRIMARY.id)",userId:"@td(LOYALTY_VIP_USER.memberId)",filter:"<product>"){ items{ id loyaltyPoints{ amount currency{ code } } } }
[API] repeat as a non-VIP / no-group customer (any non-VIP storefront user) for the same product
Assertions:
[FORM] the program saves with the condition 'User group is "VIP"' and a Product factors row (factor=6) for the product
[API] for the VIP user, product.loyaltyPoints reflects the VIP program's factor (amount > base; currency.code resolves)
[API] for the non-VIP / no-group user, product.loyaltyPoints falls back to the default (no VIP-factor applied)
[EVIDENCE] per-customer-group factor is realized as group-scoped programs: user group -> matching program -> that program's product factor; no match -> default
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] no JS errors during save
[NETWORK] no 4xx/5xx on save
[API] /api/loyalty-program-product-factors/search returns the factor row for the VIP program
Failure_Signals: Condition cannot be added or group dropdown empty; program save fails with the user-group condition; VIP and non-VIP users get identical loyaltyPoints (group scoping not applied); factor not reflected in loyaltyPoints
Cleanup: Delete the Product factors row, then delete the AGENT-TEST- VIP program (DELETE /api/loyalty-programs?ids=… cascades the factor)

Title: Per-Product Factor — Delete a factor row removes the override
Section: Loyalty > Product Points Loyalty > Product factors
Preconditions:
[PRE:CLEAR_SESSION]
VERIFIED 2026-05-29 (Loyalty 3.1003.0): Product factors live under a Product Points Loyalty program; the program's AUDIENCE is set by a rule-tree condition (Add condition > "Shopper profile" > "User group is ..." or "Any User Group"). Per-customer-group factors are modeled as ONE program per group (each program has its own Product factors); matching = customer user group -> matching program -> that program's product factor; no matching program/group -> default. Path to factors: Loyalty > Add > "Product Points Loyalty" > fill Name/Store (+ add a "User group is..."/"Any User Group" condition for audience) > Save > "Product factors" widget appears at bottom of the program blade > open it > Add > "Select Catalog items..." picker (columns Pic/Name/SKU) > check product > "Add selected" > factor grid columns Product name | SKU | Factor (spinbutton, default 1) > Save. REST: /api/loyalty-program-product-factors (model {loyaltyProgramId, productId, factor}); group lives on the program conditions, not the factor row. State from LOY-026 (a Product Points Loyalty program has a saved Product factors row, e.g. factor=2 for a test product).
Steps:
[NAV] {{BACK_URL}}/#!/workspace/loyalty
[ACT] open the 'Product Points Loyalty' program from LOY-026
[ACT] click the 'Product factors' widget to open the Product factors blade
[BLADE] the saved factor row is visible
[ACT] check the row's selection checkbox and click 'Delete' in the Product factors blade toolbar
[SAVE] click 'Save'
[ACT] click 'Refresh' (or reopen the Product factors blade)
Assertions:
[FORM] deleted factor row no longer appears in the Product factors grid
[FORM] blade saves without the row (no stale reference)
[API] /api/loyalty-program-product-factors/search no longer returns the deleted row
[EVIDENCE] product falls back to the program/default factor — no explicit override row remains
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] no JS errors
[NETWORK] no 4xx/5xx on delete/save
Failure_Signals: Row persists after delete+save; save fails after delete; 500 on delete; stale row reappears on reopen
Cleanup: Delete the AGENT-TEST- program if not already removed
